creator: adridg@freebsd.org
text:
To expand on this: in bsd,options.mk,

{option}_CMAKE_ON is added to CMAKE_ARGS when the option is on,
{option}_CMAKE_OFF is added to CMAKE_ARGS when the option is off

so the mismatch between names is a trap for the unwary. Warning on {option}_CMAKE_ARGS (which I guess people will assume does what {option}_CMAKE_ON does) is a good idea.

A minor extra check would be to examine the values assigned to {option}_CMAKE_BOOL and check that they are just words (since bsd.options.mk adds -D&:BOOL=true) that can be passed as variable names to cmake -- just in case someone does {option}_CMAKE_BOOL=-Dsomevar=true .

I'd send in a patch, but looking at the license stated in the port and the code, I don't think I'm allowed to modify it.
